assertions.text: V1 was traveling east in the first of four lanes on a two-way roadway (implies V1 was moving eastward prior to contact).
V2 was traveling east in the same lane ahead of V1 (implies V2 was moving eastward prior to contact).
The front of V1 contacted the back of V2 (rear-end impact by V1 into V2).
V2 was redirected off the right side of the road (V2's post-impact trajectory was to the right side).
The right side of V2 contacted the concrete traffic barrier (impact between V2's right side and barrier).
There was at least one distinct impact: the front of V1 contacting the back of V2.
V2 also impacted the concrete traffic barrier with its right side (a second distinct impact).
The front of V1 contacted the back of V2 before V2 was redirected off the right side of the road and struck the barrier (collision sequence: V1->V2 then V2->barrier).
Both vehicles then came to rest in the road (final rest positions of V1 and V2 were in the roadway).
V2 at final rest had a major fire (significant post-crash fire involving V2).

verdicts.evidence: V1 was moving prior to impact: [speed was 253.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s and fell to 203.0 km/h by t=-0.5 s](T3), and a detected pre-impact speed drop from [253.0 km/h to 234.0 km/h between t=-4.5 and t=-1.5 s](T4).
Telemetry is from V1 only; there are no sensors for V2's motion in this dataset, so V2's travel direction prior to contact is [unavailable in the data](T1).

Crash-pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V consistent with a frontal deceleration: [max longitudinal delta-V -88.55 km/h at t=140 ms](T1) and stats show [value at max -89.0 km/h at t=0.14 s](T2), indicating V1 experienced a strong forward-facing impact (rear-ending another vehicle).

Post-impact trajectory of V2 is not recorded by V1's sensors; lateral delta-V of V1 is [−23.0 km/h at t=0.12 s](T4) but that describes V1's lateral motion, not V2's redirection, so V2 being redirected to the right is [unverifiable from V1 data](T1).

Contact between V2's right side and a barrier is about V2 and barrier; V1 telemetry contains no direct evidence of V2's side-of-impact or barrier contact—only V1's lateral delta-V [−23.0 km/h at t=0.12 s](T4), so this assertion is [unverifiable].

Detects a crash event: the EDR summary records [num_events_recorded = 1](T1) and the crash-pulse shows a clear longitudinal delta-V with final cumulative [−86.0 km/h](T2) indicating at least one distinct impact (V1 contacting V2).

V1's telemetry does not directly record V2's subsequent impact with a barrier; while lateral delta-V in V1 is [−23.0 km/h at t=0.12 s](T4), that alone does not prove a separate barrier impact by V2, so this is [unverifiable].

Sequence on V1: braking began at [t=-4.5 s](T3) and the crash-pulse peak occurs at [t=0.14 s with longitudinal delta-V −88.55 km/h](T1), indicating V1's impact occurred at t=0 before larger post-impact motions; this supports that V1 struck ahead (V2) before later events attributed to V2 (barrier) which are not in the V1 data but sequence V1->V2 then V2->barrier is consistent with V1's recorded impact timing [t=0 to 0.14 s](T2).

Final rest positions of V1 and V2 are not recorded in V1's telemetry; the dataset includes no GPS final-rest coordinates, so the claim that both rested in the roadway is [unverifiable from available channels](T1).
Post-crash fire involving V2 is not recorded by V1's telemetry; no thermal or fire flags exist in the EDR summary, so the claim of a major fire for V2 is [unverifiable from V1 data](T1).

result.rationale: V1's telemetry supports that V1 was moving and experienced a strong frontal deceleration (rear-end impact) with large longitudinal delta-V ([−88.55 km/h peak](T1), [final −86.0 km/h](T2)). However, assertions about V2's motion, barrier impact, final rest positions, and post-crash fire involve other vehicles or scene details not recorded by V1 and are therefore unverifiable from these sensors.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
